title: Serialize and restore MsExperiment objects in interoperable file formats
text: safe way store MsExperiment objects interoperable formats.

title: Serialize and restore MsExperiment objects in interoperable file formats
text: MsStash defines framework flexible, language-agnostic import export formats mass spectrometry (MS) data objects R. MsExperimentStash package extends framework implements functionality stash (restore) MsExperiment objects.

title: Serialize and restore MsExperiment objects in interoperable file formats
text: 📦 Store/restore MsExperiment objects /portable, language agnostic storage formats (stash) 🧩 Modular design via S4 parameter classes generic methods 🔄 Fully integrated Bioconductor’s alabaster.base package.

title: Serialize and restore MsExperiment objects in interoperable file formats
Serialize and restore MsExperiment objects in interoperable file formats
text: Structured archival using HDF5 JSON (via alabaster.base)

title: Stash for MsExperimentFiles — MsExperimentFilesStash
text: MsExperiment::MsExperimentFiles class stores files (rather file names) part mass spectrometry experiment. supported stash formats MsExperimentFiles objects listed sections .

title: Stash for MsExperimentFiles — MsExperimentFilesStash
code: # S4 method for class 'MsExperimentFiles,PlainTextParam' saveMsObject(object, param, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsExperimentFiles,PlainTextParam' readMsObject(object, param, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsExperimentFiles' saveObject(x, path, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsExperimentFiles,AlabasterParam' saveMsObject(object, param, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsExperimentFiles,AlabasterParam' readMsObject(object, param, ...)

title: Stash for MsExperimentFiles — MsExperimentFilesStash
text: object MsExperimentFiles object. param MsStash::AlabasterParam MsStash::PlainTextParam. ... Currently ignored. x MsExperimentFiles object. path saveObject(): character(1) path object stored .

title: Stash for MsExperimentFiles — MsExperimentFilesStash
text: text-file format stash folder MsExperimentFile objects contains file ms_experiment_files.txt two tabulator separated columns name files. row (except first) one element MsExperimentFile, first defining object's names second content, represents characted vector file name(s), separated "|" (one).

title: Stash for MsExperimentFiles — MsExperimentFilesStash
text: MsExperimentFiles stash folder contains alabaster-specific OBJECT file sub-folder x MsExperimentFiles content serialized alabaster.base.

title: Stash for MsExperimentFiles — MsExperimentFilesStash
code: library(MsExperiment) #> Loading required package: ProtGenerics #>  #> Attaching package: ‘ProtGenerics’ #> The following object is masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     smooth  fls <- MsExperimentFiles(list(input = c("file.mzML", "file2.mgf")))  ## Define the path to the stash d <- file.path(tempdir(), "ms_file_stash")  ## Stash the object in alabaster format saveMsObject(fls, AlabasterParam(d))  ## The content of the stash: subfolder x contains the *character list* ## saved through the *alabaster.base* package. library(fs) dir_tree(d) #> /tmp/RtmplvXLPk/ms_file_stash #> ├── OBJECT #> ├── _environment.json #> └── x #>     ├── OBJECT #>     └── list_contents.json.gz  ## Restore the object from stash res <- readMsObject(MsExperimentFiles(), AlabasterParam(d)) res #> MsExperimentFiles of length  1  #> [["input"]] file.mzML file2.mgf  ## In addition, it is possible to read the object also with the ## *alabaster.base* functionality library(alabaster.base) res <- readObject(d)

title: MsExperimentStash 0.97
text: Add plain text file-based MsExperimentFiles stash.
